title: Task Envelope
description: What the launcher hands to an agent container at the start of a run, mounted read-only at /workspace/input/run-request.json.

run_id.description: Unique identifier for this run. Used for workspace paths, run history, and artifact storage.

agent_id.description: Which agent this run is for. Must match metadata.id of an agent.yaml in the registry.

target.description: The subject of the run. For browser-based agents this drives both navigation and the per-run proxy ACL.

entry_url.description: The single URL the agent starts from. Must be a well-formed http(s) URL.

scope.description: 'single-page' restricts the run (and the proxy ACL) to entry_url's exact host only. 'site' allows the launcher to derive a wildcarded ACL entry for the registrable domain (e.g. *.example.com) so the agent can crawl across subdomains of the same site during a multi-page audit.

max_pages.description: Upper bound on pages the agent may visit in one run when scope is 'site'. Ignored for 'single-page'. Required when scope is 'site' so a crawl can't run unbounded.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the run was requested.

parameters.description: Agent-specific extras. Open-ended; each agent defines and validates its own expected keys.
